evidence: `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts` exposes multiple broad state surfaces (`InlineEditStateResult`, `InlineEditCanvasState`, `InlineEditSidebarState`) and then `useInlineEditViewState` re-slices the same object, creating extra indirection rather than reducing complexity.

`src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx` mostly forwards state/handlers from `useInlineEditViewState` into child components (`InlineEditCanvas`, `InlineEditSidebar`, `EditModePanel`), indicating wrapper-heavy handoff layers.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` and `useVideoLightboxController.ts` import `VideoLightboxProps` from `../VideoLightbox` while `VideoLightbox.tsx` imports those hooks, and both hooks use placeholder media objects for form-only flows (`{ id: '__segment-form-only__' }` and `({} as GenerationRow)`), showing contract pressure from over-coupled abstractions.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`, and `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts` repeat the same multi-step payload choreography (`buildOrchestrationContract` + `buildTaskViewContract` + family contract + `composeTaskPayload`) with large option bags and conditional field spreading, increasing abstraction overhead and drift risk.

findings.summary: Inline edit flow uses oversized state contracts and pass-through selectors
evidence: `useInlineEditState` returns a very large nested object (`canvasEnvironment`, `inpaintingState`, `transformState`, `generationState`, `imageEditValue`) and also defines additional projection types (`InlineEditCanvasState`, `InlineEditSidebarState`).

`useInlineEditViewState` immediately calls `useInlineEditState` and remaps fields via `selectInlineEditCanvasState`/`selectInlineEditSidebarState`, adding an extra wrapper layer without reducing dependency surface.
`InlineEditView` mostly forwards this mapped state to `InlineEditCanvas`/`InlineEditSidebar` and then to `EditModePanel`, preserving high coupling across the chain.
findings.suggestion: Refactor inline edit to expose a smaller command-oriented API per concern (canvas, generation actions, sidebar actions) and let leaf components consume dedicated hooks/context directly. Remove `useInlineEditViewState` as a pass-through layer once slices are independently consumable.

findings.summary: VideoLightbox hooks depend on component types and rely on fake media objects
evidence: `useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` and `useVideoLightboxController.ts` import `VideoLightboxProps` from `../VideoLightbox` while `VideoLightbox.tsx` imports those hooks, creating a tight bidirectional dependency at the type boundary.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` fabricates a placeholder generation (`{ id: '__segment-form-only__' }`) for form-only mode.
`useVideoLightboxController` uses `const fallbackMedia = media || ({} as GenerationRow)` to satisfy downstream APIs, which hides an invalid state behind type assertion.
findings.suggestion: Move shared prop/model types into a neutral `types` module and model form-only mode as a discriminated union so media-required paths cannot be called without real media. Remove placeholder generation objects and explicit casts by splitting media and form-only render pipelines.

findings.summary: Task payload assembly repeats a high-ceremony contract pipeline across task families
evidence: Multiple task modules build raw param objects, then separately call `buildOrchestrationContract`, `buildTaskViewContract`, family-specific contract builders, and finally `composeTaskPayload`.

`payloadBuilder.ts` contains a long conditional spread-based orchestrator payload object before contract composition; `joinClips.ts` and `individualTravelSegment.ts` repeat similar assembly responsibilities with different field names.
This repeated orchestration contract choreography means each schema evolution requires synchronized edits in many files, indicating abstraction cost outweighing reuse.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared task-family payload composer that accepts a typed family schema and standard lifecycle metadata, so modules provide only family-specific fields and validation. Centralize contract assembly and default propagation to reduce per-file boilerplate and drift.

findings.summary: Lightbox layout relies on wide controller bags that mostly relay handlers
evidence: `LightboxLayoutControllers`/`LayoutWorkflowBarProps` define very broad prop bags (numerous callbacks, refs, and workflow flags) rather than focused interfaces.

`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` constructs and forwards large layout/controller structures to `LightboxLayout` (`layoutProps` + `controllers`), and `ImageLightbox` follows the same controller assembly style.
Most fields are passed through to child controls (`WorkflowControlsBar`, button groups, navigation components), increasing indirection and coupling between orchestration hooks and view primitives.
findings.suggestion: Decompose `LightboxLayout` into smaller feature components with narrow props (workflow actions, navigation, panel controls) and move state access closer to consumers through dedicated hooks/context selectors instead of top-level controller bags.
